title: CBP Agents Shoot Two in Portland; DHS Account Immediately Contradicted
excerpt: U.S. Customs and Border Protection agents shot and wounded two Venezuelan immigrants outside a Portland, Oregon medical clinic on January 8, 2026. DHS initially claimed an agent fired 'a defensive shot,' a claim witnesses and later evidence contradicted.
content:
On January 8, 2026 — one day after the fatal shooting of Renée Good in Minneapolis — CBP agents conducting a targeted vehicle stop in Portland's Hazelwood neighborhood shot and wounded two people, Luis David Nico-Moncada and Yorlenys Betzabeth Zambrano-Contreras.

DHS first claimed the agent fired 'a defensive shot,' but DHS Secretary McLaughlin later revised the account to multiple 'shots.' One witness reported hearing five gunshots. Emergency personnel confirmed the man suffered two gunshot wounds, contradicting the initial federal account. None of the six Border Patrol agents present wore body cameras.

  • DHS alleged Nico-Moncada 'weaponized his vehicle,' echoing the language used after the Minneapolis shooting the day before.
  • Portland Mayor Keith Wilson called the event 'deeply troubling' and demanded ICE halt all city operations pending investigation.
  • Oregon Attorney General opened a formal investigation into whether any federal officer acted outside their lawful authority.
  • The FBI announced it was 'investigating an agent-involved shooting,' but noted as of January 10 there was no body-worn camera footage and no surveillance footage had been found.
"Portland is not a training ground for militarized agents, and the 'full force' threatened by the administration has deadly consequences." — Portland Mayor Keith Wilson
